jokes.text: Well, then I guess cocaine is weightless, all right? I'm gonna file that up here actually.
jokes.setup: Characters struggling to weigh cocaine on a scale
jokes.punchline: Charlie concludes cocaine must be weightless and files this as scientific fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - he creates his own delusional scientific theory. Strong character integration with his absurdist worldview.

jokes.text: What are you, a cat? Use a towel. My mouth's right here.
jokes.setup: Charlie cleaning cocaine off his hands
jokes.punchline: Charlie suggests using his mouth instead of a towel
jokes.explanation: Disgusting Charlie behavior that's in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We have to be very careful, considering our past addictions to glue and crack.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not getting high on their drug supply
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of their specific bizarre addiction history
jokes.explanation: Casually referencing their pathetic addiction history as if it's normal

jokes.text: Every ho needs his pimp.
jokes.setup: Frank complaining Charlie didn't contribute to earning money
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely inappropriate comparison of their partnership
jokes.explanation: Brief but perfectly captures Charlie's warped worldview and inappropriate analogies

jokes.text: Waltz into a board meeting, slap your drugs down on the conference table... and be, like, 'Hey, you guys wanna buy drugs? We're drug dealers.'
jokes.setup: Mac explaining how to sell cocaine to rich people
jokes.punchline: His plan is to literally announce they're drug dealers at a board meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Mac's delusional confidence and terrible planning skills.

jokes.text: It's a rich person's drug. There's no danger involved. It's, like, stockbrokers and high-powered business executives.
jokes.setup: Someone warning that drug dealing is dangerous
jokes.punchline: Mac's completely naive understanding of cocaine's customer base
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusional confidence about drug dealing safety based on TV stereotypes

jokes.text: I am not a prostitute. I am providing... Let me handle this. Dennis is providing a very important service to lonely people in need.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to deny he's a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Frank cuts him off to describe prostitution in euphemistic terms
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dennis's denial and Frank's manipulation.

jokes.text: Oh, no beer. Too many calories. Yep. It's all about keeping this body fit. Mm-hmm. Ehheh. I'll get you a nice vodka tonic.
jokes.setup: Frank refusing Dennis beer due to calories
jokes.punchline: Offers vodka tonic as a healthier alternative while pimping him out
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities during prostitution, but not exceptionally crafted.

jokes.text: Every time you talk, you weaken the nation. Shut up.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to contribute to mob conversation
jokes.punchline: Mobster delivers devastating insult with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Exceptional line - perfect economy and devastating delivery. Highly quotable and earned through Mac's incompetence.

jokes.text: It's, like, the perfect mix of comfort and wind resistance. They make that In men's?
jokes.setup: Mac admiring what appear to be women's tracksuits
jokes.punchline: Asks if they make them in men's sizes after praising their aerodynamics
jokes.explanation: Mac's oblivious masculinity and focus on odd details is well-executed.

jokes.text: I'm so hard that people are scared of me. And they should be, 'cause I'll explode all over them.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to prove he's tough to mobsters
jokes.punchline: Accidentally uses sexual innuendo while describing his toughness
jokes.explanation: Mac's obliviousness to sexual innuendo while trying to be intimidating is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That's right. Hands like a cat. You know what? I think someone just earned themselves a nickname. From now on, you'll be known as Pussy Hands.
jokes.setup: Mac claiming to have catlike reflexes
jokes.punchline: Mobsters give him the humiliating nickname 'Pussy Hands'
jokes.explanation: Great setup and payoff - Mac's attempt to sound tough backfires spectacularly.

jokes.text: I'd like to go on record saying I don't like Pussy Hands. But Cougar Hands would be good.
jokes.setup: Mac got stuck with embarrassing nickname
jokes.punchline: He tries to negotiate for a cooler cat-based nickname
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Mac's inability to accept his situation while maintaining his delusions.

jokes.text: I'd like to go on record saying I don't like Pussy Hands. But Cougar Hands would be good.
jokes.setup: Mac getting the degrading nickname
jokes.punchline: Still trying to negotiate for a slightly less embarrassing cat-based nickname
jokes.explanation: Mac's pathetic attempt to salvage dignity by negotiating for slightly better cat nickname

jokes.text: How about Jaguar Hands? Nah. Leopard Hands? Nah. Panther Hands. Nah.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to negotiate his nickname
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate suggestions all getting immediately shot down
jokes.explanation: Escalating desperation as each 'cooler' cat name gets rejected

jokes.text: Go get your saucer of milk, honey. He does have gorgeous hands though.
jokes.setup: The Pussy Hands nickname established
jokes.punchline: Treating Mac like an actual cat while giving backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Extending the cat joke while adding weird sexual undertone

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure those jockeys are raping that horse. They're raping the shit out of it.
jokes.setup: Charlie talking about horse racing
jokes.punchline: He interprets horse racing as sexual assault
jokes.explanation: Dark and absurd in a way that's quintessentially Charlie - disturbing logic delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Rub it all over your gums, Dee. All over your gums. It's like my mouth is no longer a part of my head.
jokes.setup: Charlie teaching Dee how to use cocaine without snorting
jokes.punchline: Dee describes the numbing sensation in disturbing terms
jokes.explanation: Gross drug behavior that's in character but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: A little dip into our Bolivian marching powder, then we're back on the street.
jokes.setup: Need to boost their energy for drug dealing
jokes.punchline: Charlie's overly elaborate euphemism for cocaine
jokes.explanation: Moderately clever euphemism but not particularly original or character-specific

jokes.text: Rickety Cricket! That's Rickety Cricket! Rickety! Rickety Cricket! Rickety!
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee high on cocaine encounter Cricket
jokes.punchline: They repeatedly chant his nickname like excited children
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how drugs make them regress to childlike behavior.

jokes.text: Ever since you convinced me to abandon the church... my life has been in a bit of a tailspin. That doesn't ring a bell.
jokes.setup: Cricket explaining his downfall to Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee completely denies responsibility for ruining his life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee behavior - callously denying her role in someone's destruction.

jokes.text: We would do it, but we're not street urchins. Yeah, man. Yeah. Know what I mean? But you. They would buy drugs from a street urchin like yourself.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Cricket to sell drugs for them
jokes.punchline: They repeatedly call the former priest a 'street urchin'
jokes.explanation: Brutal dehumanization of Cricket delivered with casual cruelty that's perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I guess Charlie and I didn't see you sitting atop your homeless ivory tower.
jokes.setup: Cricket refusing to sell drugs
jokes.punchline: Dee sarcastically references his 'homeless ivory tower'
jokes.explanation: Excellent oxymoron that perfectly captures Dee's cruel wit and the absurdity of shaming a homeless person for having standards.

jokes.text: Every pimp has a chalice. This one is the shit. You spend a shitload of money on a chalice you're just gonna eat cereal out of...
jokes.setup: Frank showing off an expensive pimp chalice
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out he'll just use it for cereal
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Frank's grandiose pimp fantasy and mundane reality. Well-structured setup and payoff.

jokes.text: You spend a shitload of money on a chalice you're just gonna eat cereal out of... but you won't bail us out by paying the mob?
jokes.setup: Frank's expensive pimp chalice
jokes.punchline: Mac pointing out Frank's screwed-up priorities
jokes.explanation: Good logical contrast showing Frank's absurd spending priorities and selfishness.

jokes.text: Sixty-eight tops. That's still extremely old. Well, I'm not kissing that.
jokes.setup: Discussing the age of Dennis's elderly client
jokes.punchline: Dennis draws the line at kissing despite being a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's arbitrary standards and denial about his situation.

jokes.text: This time, nothing with the ass. That got really weird last time.
jokes.setup: Dennis setting rules for his prostitution
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to previous disturbing sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Dennis trying to maintain dignity while discussing degrading sexual acts

jokes.text: Hypothetically speaking here... do you think you could teach a horse to sit on a barstool and drink beer?
jokes.setup: Charlie asking a seemingly random question
jokes.punchline: Reveals his ongoing obsession with getting a horse for the bar
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Charlie logic - the specificity of the image and his serious delivery makes this exceptional character comedy.

jokes.text: Look at this place. It's exactly as I imagined it would be. Hey, is there a dream catcher above your bed?
jokes.setup: Cricket entering Dee's apartment
jokes.punchline: He immediately spots stereotypical single woman decor
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Dee's predictable apartment aesthetic.

jokes.text: This is more money than we make at the bar. I have never seen this much money in one spot.
jokes.setup: Seeing the money Cricket made from drug sales
jokes.punchline: Admission that their legitimate business is a complete failure
jokes.explanation: Shows how pathetic their bar business is compared to drug dealing success.

jokes.text: I can get a horse. You're not gonna get a horse, Charlie. We can have some coke then. She likes Swiss! I knew it! I pictured Swiss.
jokes.setup: Discussion of spending drug money
jokes.punchline: Charlie pivots from horse to cocaine to confirming Dee has Swiss cheese
jokes.explanation: Charlie's stream-of-consciousness logic and inexplicable knowledge about Dee's cheese preferences is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: We say things all day. We say words all the time. We said so many things.
jokes.setup: Cricket complaining they said it was one-time
jokes.punchline: Dee dismisses all promises as meaningless with existential absurdity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nihilistic dismissal of accountability. The escalating absurdity of denying language itself has meaning is peak Sunny.

jokes.text: No more dinners. We're going straight to bangin' from now on.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking about dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Frank cuts out any pretense of escort service
jokes.explanation: Frank's blunt elimination of Dennis's last shred of dignity is in character but not exceptionally crafted.

jokes.text: It says right here... 'No Rules.' Don't be so uptight.
jokes.setup: Dennis referencing his escort rules
jokes.punchline: Frank's flyer literally says 'No Rules'
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag showing Frank's complete betrayal of Dennis's boundaries in the most literal way possible.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get you out of this. It's you and me against the world. You're my one and only. You gotta do right by me, okay?
jokes.setup: Frank hitting Dennis
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to manipulative affection like an abusive pimp
jokes.explanation: Dark portrayal of abusive relationship dynamics with Frank's quick emotional manipulation.

jokes.text: Is it any crazier than having a dog that bites or sheds? Or a cat that poops in a box while it's biting you?
jokes.setup: Mac saying getting a horse is crazy
jokes.punchline: Charlie compares horses to bizarrely aggressive household pets
jokes.explanation: Charlie's warped logic about pet ownership is perfectly absurd - the specificity of simultaneous pooping and biting is brilliant.

jokes.text: I'm writing a musical. It's about life on the streets. Archangel has to live on the streets and fight crime.
jokes.setup: Cricket explaining what he's been doing
jokes.punchline: He's writing a homeless superhero musical while high on drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd combination of Cricket's delusions of grandeur, drug use, and current homeless situation.

jokes.text: You 'sold' our drug money on two garbage cans? These are trash cans, Cricket! These are trash cans? Then why do they sound like this?
jokes.setup: Cricket bought 'kettledrums' with drug money
jokes.punchline: He's confused when told they're garbage cans because they make sound
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Cricket's drug-fueled delusions and his inability to distinguish instruments from trash.

jokes.text: I don't think I'm being properly utilized within the organization.
jokes.setup: Mac talking to the mob boss about his role
jokes.punchline: Using corporate HR language to discuss mob hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - applying business school concepts to organized crime.

jokes.text: There's gotta be a union boss I can put the squeeze on. Or a truck I can hijack, right?
jokes.setup: Mac complaining about being underutilized in the mob
jokes.punchline: Mac's movie-based understanding of organized crime
jokes.explanation: Mac's naive movie-inspired ideas about organized crime work

jokes.text: There's gotta be a union boss I can put the squeeze on. Or a truck I can hijack, right?
jokes.setup: Mac wanting to do gangster work
jokes.punchline: Mac's movie-based understanding of mob activities
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's completely stereotypical and unrealistic view of organized crime from movies.

jokes.text: You mean, like, rub her out? No. Oh. You want me to bang her? I'm sorry. No! What the hell is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Don Sal asking Mac to 'take care of' his wife
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately assumes murder then sex instead of housework
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - his mind immediately goes to extreme violence then sex. Great misdirection and character work.

jokes.text: You mean, like, rub her out? No. Oh. You want me to bang her?
jokes.setup: Mob boss asking Mac to 'take care of' his wife
jokes.punchline: Mac's interpretations going from murder to sex instead of the obvious (housework)
jokes.explanation: Mac's mind going to extremes while missing the simple domestic task

jokes.text: Frank, he cannot bang this woman. It's the mob boss's wife.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to stop Dennis from prostituting himself
jokes.punchline: Mac's concern is about the client's identity, not the prostitution itself
jokes.explanation: Mac's misplaced priorities - worried about practical consequences not moral ones

jokes.text: Frank, he cannot bang this woman. It's the mob boss's wife. What do you think she's gonna do... call her husband and say she's banging a whore?
jokes.setup: Mac warning about sleeping with the mob boss's wife
jokes.punchline: Frank points out the obvious flaw in this concern
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic that reveals the absurdity of the situation - of course she won't report her own infidelity.

jokes.text: Me? Oh, no. I would never sleep with you. You're gross.
jokes.setup: Mob boss's wife asking if Mac would sleep with her
jokes.punchline: Mac's blunt honesty to dangerous mob wife
jokes.explanation: Mac's dangerous honesty showing complete lack of social awareness

jokes.text: Me? Oh, no. I would never sleep with you. You're gross.
jokes.setup: Mob wife asking if Mac wants to sleep with her
jokes.punchline: Mac brutally rejects her while everyone's in danger
jokes.explanation: Mac's tactless honesty at the worst possible moment is perfectly in character and escalates the danger.

jokes.text: Who's Peter Nincompoop? I rode it here. What do you think?
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions Peter Nincompoop
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be the name of his horse
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Charlie's horse obsession with an absurdly specific name. The casual reveal that he actually got the horse is brilliant.

jokes.text: How did that get here? I rode it here. What do you think?
jokes.setup: Surprise appearance of actual horse
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact explanation that he rode it there
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Charlie's horse obsession - he actually got one and casually mentions riding it to the bar.

jokes.text: Go, Peter Nincompoop! I love you, Peter Nincompoop.
jokes.setup: Charlie sending his horse away
jokes.punchline: Emotional goodbye to a horse with a ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Charlie's genuine emotion for his absurdly named horse creates perfect pathos within the comedy.

jokes.text: Go, Peter Nincompoop! I love you, Peter Nincompoop.
jokes.setup: Charlie sending his horse away
jokes.punchline: Charlie's emotional goodbye to his ridiculously named horse
jokes.explanation: Good emotional moment showing Charlie's genuine affection for his absurdly named horse.

jokes.text: And when I ask for a light, we come out blastin'! Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom! No! No! We are not gonna come out blasting!
jokes.setup: Charlie planning elaborate gun fight with mobsters
jokes.punchline: Mac's panicked rejection of Charlie's violent fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Charlie's action movie fantasy and Mac's realistic fear of actual gangsters.

jokes.text: Where'd you get the money? I sold Frank's pimp chalice. What? That's right, you son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Mac paying off the mob debt
jokes.punchline: He sold Frank's expensive chalice to save them
jokes.explanation: Great callback and poetic justice - Frank's ridiculous pimp accessory saves them from his prostitution scheme.

jokes.text: You guys, you gotta make it sexy... hips and nips. Otherwise, I'm not eatin'.
jokes.setup: Random Charlie interjection during tense mob scene
jokes.punchline: He demands the mobsters dance sexily or he won't eat
jokes.explanation: Legendary Charlie absurdity - completely inappropriate demand during dangerous situation. 'Hips and nips' became iconic.

jokes.text: I did it! And I made It so sexy! Look at this. I've achieved total sexiness! I am the man! I made it so sexy.
jokes.setup: Cricket confessing to sleeping with the mob wife
jokes.punchline: He's proud of his 'sexiness' while about to be killed
jokes.explanation: Cricket's delusional pride in his 'sexiness' while signing his death warrant is perfect dark comedy.

jokes.text: They broke my legs but they didn't break my spirit / But I don't feel no pain 'cause I found more cocaine
jokes.setup: Cricket after being beaten by the mob
jokes.punchline: Singing optimistic song about his injuries while celebrating finding drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy ending - Cricket's broken optimism and drug addiction as musical triumph

jokes.text: They broke my legs but they didn't break my spirit. But I don't feel no pain 'cause I found more cocaine. Cocaine
jokes.setup: Cricket after being beaten by the mob
jokes.punchline: He sings about cocaine solving his problems despite broken legs
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to Cricket's musical ambitions, now twisted by his complete downfall and drug addiction.
